lesson.menu_title: Used To — Past Habits and Routines
lesson.menu_desc: Talk about how things were before but are not true now — used to + base verb for old habits, routines and systems.
lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 — Used To: Past Habits and Routines
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Daniela Feitoza | Aula 16 -- Used To: Past Habits and Routines

listenings.text: Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Today, listen to one short voice note about used to, the structure for past habits and routines. Use it for something that was true before but is not true now. I used to work with the old legacy system every day. We used to do everything by hand, with paper and spreadsheets. The store used to close at six, and the team used to count the stock manually. Now, here is the key. The form is always used to plus the base verb. Say I used to work, never I used to working, and never I use to work in the past. For questions and negatives, the d disappears. Did you use to work late? No, I did not use to work on weekends. Remember, used to talks about a habit that has changed, so it always points to a contrast with nowadays. Try it. Think about your old routine at work and how it is different now. Talk soon.

Hello Daniela, this is Marcus. I am leaving you a voicemail about the system migration project. So, I was thinking about how much our routine has changed. Two years ago, we used to work with the old legacy system, and it used to crash almost every week. The team used to update the prices by hand, store by store, on a huge spreadsheet. We used to spend three full days on the monthly report. We did not use to trust the data, honestly. Then, gradually, we started to automate everything. Now the new platform does it in minutes, and nobody uses paper anymore. Funny, right? We used to complain about the old way, but we also used to know every step by heart. Nowadays it is faster, but I think we should still document the manual process for new people. Call me back when you can. Talk soon, Daniela.
